chief_complaint: A 22-year-old male with an acute cervical spinal cord injury (C4) and multiple subsegmental pulmonary emboli.
history: The patient underwent regular mechanical insufflation-exsufflation (MI-E) and manual assisted coughs while therapeutically anticoagulated with enoxaparin. Contributing factors included a higher dose of therapeutic anticoagulation due to an elevated body mass index and a requirement for frequent, firm manual assisted coughs.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: ECG, bloods, and imaging results not provided.
